short_text: Naseem Shah to van Meekeren, van Meekeren Run Out!! 1 run completed slower ball that was pitched up, driven down to long
full_text: Naseem Shah to van Meekeren, van Meekeren Run Out!! 1 run completed slower ball that was pitched up, driven down to long-on and the batters attempt a suicidal second run only to fall short. It was the last ball of the innings anyway. The Dutch close out with 91 runs, I'd reckon they are a few runs short here...

full_text: Naseem Shah to van Beek, 1 run, another slower bouncer, pulled to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Naseem Shah to van Meekeren, leg byes, 1 run, length ball chasing the batter, Van Meekeren looks to pull but this one strikes his body and goes behind square

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to van Meekeren, 3 runs, another yorker, this time slightly wider. Van Meekeren was ready and smokes it over covers, but the big boundaries mean that the man from sweeper has enough time to cut it off. Three runs taken!

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to van Meekeren, 2 runs, full and quick on off, Van Meekeren delightfully turns this towards deep backward point.

short_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to van Meekeren, no run, so, so close!! Another one bang in the blockhole, but this one just sways pas
full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to van Meekeren, no run, so, so close!! Another one bang in the blockhole, but this one just sways past the leg stump. Wasim Jr pleads the umpire to atleast give him a caught behind, but he'll have to wait another day to get a hat-trick

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Fred Klaassen, Bowled!! How easy was that? Wasim Jr nails the yorker once again, and although Klaassen was ready in his stance for that delivery, he can't keep that out. Off stump pegged back and

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Tim Pringle, Bowled!! You miss, I hit! Full and swinging in from Wasim Jr, Pringle shuffles away this time for a big heave, exposing all his stumps and making it too easy for the Pakistani quick.

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Tim Pringle, , full but strays down leg, this time the umpire wides!

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to van Beek, leg byes, 1 run, a superb yorker on off, just about fended away with his boots towards third-man

full_text: Shaheen Afridi to Tim Pringle, no run, just an inch away from missing the leg stump!! Once again Pringle shuffled accross for a big shot but couldn't get a connection on his glance

full_text: Haris Rauf to van Beek, no run, 149 clicks! Full and quick on off, Van Beek defends

short_text: Haris Rauf to van der Merwe, Bowled!! Poles!! I0$ fizzing in to the top of the stumps. Van der Merwe gives himself room
full_text: Haris Rauf to van der Merwe, Bowled!! Poles!! I0$ fizzing in to the top of the stumps. Van der Merwe gives himself room once again for a big heave over covers, but he misses and Haris was always going to hit! The Dutch just can't get any momentum to their innings.

full_text: Haris Rauf to Tim Pringle, 1 run, full on off, driven hard but straight to mid-off. Just a single!

full_text: Haris Rauf to van der Merwe, 1 run, quick and full on leg, the batter shuffles away to drive towards covers. A bit of a fumble allows them to cross over

full_text: Naseem Shah to Tim Pringle, no run, back of a length on leg, Pringle shuffled across to scoop it away but didn't even get close to making contact!

full_text: Naseem Shah to Tim Pringle, no run, fuller on off, blocked into the pitch and Pringle wanted the quick single but the backward point fielder was alert

full_text: Naseem Shah to Edwards, Caught by Iftikhar Ahmed!! Pitched up and a tad slower, Edwards tries to scoop it very fine but that wasn't the best choice with a fielder at fine leg. Iftikhar the man who runs in from the boundary to grab onto a good low catch. The Dutch captain walks back as Naseem has his first wicket of the day!

full_text: Naseem Shah to van der Merwe, 1 run, full and chasing the batter who shuffled away. Van der Merwe taps and runs towards mid-on

full_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, no run, Edwards attempts the fine paddle once again, this time the ball lobs off his pads and goes just short of the fine leg fielder who was in the circle

full_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, , smart paddle sweep from Edwards. Takes it from off and plays it very fine, to the left of the fielder in the circle to get a welcome boundary

full_text: Shadab Khan to van der Merwe, 3 runs, first triple for the Dutch! Van der Merwe swings a little harder on this pitched up delivery to get it past the mid-wicket fielder. In the gap between deep square leg and long-on to pinch three runs

full_text: Shadab Khan to Ackermann, Lbw!! Ackermann tried to reverse sweep Shadab but didn't get any bat on that. Struck on his pad, in line with the stumps and the ball would have crashed into the top of off. Netherlands lose their second review as Shadab continues to shine!

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Edwards, leg byes, 1 run, on a length around middle and leg, Edwards looks to clip this away, it comes off the pads and rolls to square leg

full_text: Shadab Khan to Ackermann, , Shimmies down the wicket and swings hard at this quicker delivery, ends up getting an outside edge that flies through the vacant slip cordon, beats the fielder at short third man and races away to the fence

full_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, 1 run, goes for a sweep, gets an underedge towards short fine leg

short_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, no run, Is the impact in line? Or is the impact outside off? I think the latter, but let's wait
full_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, no run, Is the impact in line? Or is the impact outside off? I think the latter, but let's wait for the replays. It was the googly by the leg-spinner and Edwards got beaten as he tried to flick this across the line. Beaten due to the wrong 'un, but the impact is well outside off, like I anticipated. Even Rizwan was pretty sure the impact wasn't in line. Pakistan and Netherlands - both have lost a review each

short_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, no run, fraction short outside off, Edwards opens the bat face, guides it to short third man
full_text: Shadab Khan to Edwards, no run, fraction short outside off, Edwards opens the bat face, guides it to short third man

short_text: Shadab Khan to Max ODowd, Lbw!! He has been adjudged LBW by the onfield umpire, and he has decided to challenge the deci
full_text: Shadab Khan to Max ODowd, Lbw!! He has been adjudged LBW by the onfield umpire, and he has decided to challenge the decision. Nothing on UltraEdge, it's a flat line, so no bat involved. And Hawkeye confirms three reds as well. Pitching in line, impact in line and it's crashing into the leg-stump. So that's plumb and Max ODowd will have to make his way back to the pavilion. Was a flatter delivery around middle and leg, and the batter got beaten as he tried to work this across the line. Netherlands sink further as they lose their third wicket, with hardly anything on the scoreboard. Pakistan on top, at least for now! Netherlands have lost a review, they have only one review left now.

short_text: Mohammad Nawaz to Ackermann, no run, makes room and flat-bats this back to the bowler
full_text: Mohammad Nawaz to Ackermann, no run, makes room and flat-bats this back to the bowler

full_text: Mohammad Nawaz to Max ODowd, 1 run, on the pads as the batter glances it to short fine leg for a quick single. The throw at the keeper's end was a flat one and Rizwan was quick to dislodge the bails too, but a direct-hit would have made things interesting

full_text: Mohammad Nawaz to Ackermann, 1 run, shimmies down the wicket and drills this straight past the bowler, a single taken as the fielder from long-off cuts it off

full_text: Shadab Khan to Ackermann, 1 run, floated around middle and leg, Ackermann clips this through the leg-side for a single

short_text: Shadab Khan to Tom Cooper, Caught by Mohammad Wasim Jr!! Shadab Khan strikes straightaway. A short ball by the spinner a
full_text: Shadab Khan to Tom Cooper, Caught by Mohammad Wasim Jr!! Shadab Khan strikes straightaway. A short ball by the spinner and Tom Cooper pulls it straight to the fielder at deep mid-wicket. A comfortable catch taken by Wasim Jr and Tom Cooper departs without doing much. Just a single to his name and Netherlands are two down. Technically three down with de Leede retired hurt.

short_text: Haris Rauf to Tom Cooper, 1 run, low full toss on middle and leg, Tom Cooper clips it to wide mid-on and takes a single
full_text: Haris Rauf to Tom Cooper, 1 run, low full toss on middle and leg, Tom Cooper clips it to wide mid-on and takes a single to get off the mark

short_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, A fast, short ball by Haris Rauf and the batter gets surprised due to the bounce. Ta
full_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, A fast, short ball by Haris Rauf and the batter gets surprised due to the bounce. Takes his eyes off the ball and tries to swipe it away, but gets hit on the helmet. Immediately takes off the helmet does Bas de Leede. The Pakistani players rush up to him and have a check, and the physio is out in the middle too. Was bowled at 142 clicks, hopefully Bas de Leede is fine. In fact, He got a top edge that deflected on to the helmet grille. Looks like there's a slight cut on his cheek, right below the right eye

full_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, on a length around off, Bas de Leede knocks this down the pitch

full_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, hits the deck on a good length, 143kph, decent carry as Bas de Leede tries to drive it on the rise, connects thin air

full_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, skiddy length ball around middle and leg, Bas de Leede looks to work this through the leg-side, gets an inside edge on to the thigh pad

full_text: Haris Rauf to Bas de Leede, no run, on a length around middle and off, Bas de Leede brings his bat down in time, knocks it down the pitch

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Bas de Leede, no run, short of a length around middle and leg, Bas de Leede tries to whip it away, mistimes it to mid-wicket

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Bas de Leede, no run, and now he makes room to crash this through cover, but the length ball sails over the leg stump

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Bas de Leede, no run, short and quick outside off, Bas de Leede gets beaten due to the carry

full_text: Mohammad Wasim Jr to Bas de Leede, , short ball outside off, Bas de Leede rides the bounce and carves this away uppishly to the square third man boundary. There was a fielder in the deep, but Bas de Leede placed this well past him

short_text: Naseem Shah to Bas de Leede, no run, Was a short ball outside off and it seamed away from the batter. Bas de Leede pokes
full_text: Naseem Shah to Bas de Leede, no run, Was a short ball outside off and it seamed away from the batter. Bas de Leede pokes at it once again, and connects thin air, beaten due to the movement again. Rizwan wasn't that interested, hence they don't opt for the review either

full_text: Naseem Shah to Bas de Leede, no run, seaming away from the batter outside off, keeps it short, Bas de Leede has a swing and miss, played it a touch early and got beaten due to the movement off the deck as well

full_text: Naseem Shah to Max ODowd, 2 runs, pitched up by Naseem Shah around off, Max ODowd chips it over the infield at cover and takes a couple of runs

short_text: Shaheen Afridi to Myburgh, Caught by Mohammad Wasim Jr!! Afridi draws first blood! A short and quick delivery around off
full_text: Shaheen Afridi to Myburgh, Caught by Mohammad Wasim Jr!! Afridi draws first blood! A short and quick delivery around off, Myburgh looks to pull it away, gets cramped up for room, and ends up getting a top edge that flies straight over the keeper's head. Wasim Jr from fine leg sprints to his left, settles under the skier and takes it safely. Up go both his arms, typical celebrations of the Afridis..

short_text: Shaheen Afridi to Myburgh, , full and wide outside off, Myburgh frees his arms and crashes it through cover for the firs
full_text: Shaheen Afridi to Myburgh, , full and wide outside off, Myburgh frees his arms and crashes it through cover for the first boundary of the match. Hit that really hard

full_text: Naseem Shah to Max ODowd, no run, 136kph, short and quick outside off, shaping away from the batter, Max ODowd plays away from his body, and almost nicks it behind. Got beaten due to the movement in the air

full_text: Brandon Glover to Masood, Caught by van Meekeren!! With just one run to get, Masood perishes. Short and wide, Masood throws his hands at it, and it flies off a thick edge straight to third man.

full_text: van Meekeren to Iftikhar Ahmed, 1 run, brilliant save! This was cut hard and it was travelling. On top of that van Beek was standing in quite close at backward point. But he shows sharp reflexes diving to his left to get a hand on it

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 1 run, has that been taken? . Oh the front-on replay suggests that it might've hit the ground.
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 1 run, has that been taken? . Oh the front-on replay suggests that it might've hit the ground. Now there's a zoomed up replay from side-on, and the third umpire says "fingers under the ball". The soft signal is out, remember. Now we go back to the front-on replay, and finally the third umpire concludes that the ball has bounced before going into the hands. Netherlands players shake their heads as NOT OUT flashes on the screen. It was a short delivery into the body, Rizwan pulled it in the air and it was dying on the fielder who came running in from the square leg boundary. Based on the evidence, it was pretty clear the ball was touching the ground

full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, no run, back of a length outside off, there's width on offer for Rizwan who cuts it straight to backward point

full_text: Brandon Glover to Fakhar Zaman, Caught by Edwards!! Oh good take from Edwards, full stretch to his left! The edge was flying as Zaman swung hard at this full delivery outside off. And Edwards flew with it, grabbing it with both hands. Netherlands have a second wicket.

full_text: Tim Pringle to Fakhar Zaman, , drifting away outside off from round the wicket, it's quite full and lures Zaman into the drive. He gets a genuine edge but there's no slip in place and it runs away to the boundary

full_text: Tim Pringle to Rizwan, 1 run, drifting into the pads, Rizwan nudges it through midwicket for a single. Zaman turns around looking for a second but a quick call of "no" has him turning back and he puts in a full-stretch dive into the crease. He seems to have hurt himself in the process and requires the physio's attention

full_text: van Meekeren to Rizwan, 1 run, lucky!! Short pitched just outside off and Rizwan looked to pull, didn't get any timing and he thought he was gone. Just to the left of a diving mid-wicket fielder. Rizwan will bat on.

full_text: van Meekeren to Babar Azam, Babar Azam Run Out!! Fuller and straight, driven straight to mid-on and Babar hurries for the single. There's a direct hit and the Pakistani skipper is short of his ground! His lean patch continues, this time a run-out gets him!

full_text: van Meekeren to Babar Azam, no run, on a length on off, dabbed behind point where the fielder makes a leaping stop to his left. Logan van Beek the fielder

full_text: van Meekeren to Rizwan, 1 run, back of a length just outside off, cut behind the point fielder to give the strike to his captain for the first time!

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 2 runs, length ball on off, pushed through cover point for another couple. A good, confident st
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 2 runs, length ball on off, pushed through cover point for another couple. A good, confident start from Rizwan after too paltry scores

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 2 runs, full and shaping onto the boots, glanced behind square straight to the fielder this tim
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 2 runs, full and shaping onto the boots, glanced behind square straight to the fielder this time but the boundaries are big here! Another easy double

short_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, , low full toss on off, too easy for Rizwan who slams it down the ground. Mid-off is too wide a
full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, , low full toss on off, too easy for Rizwan who slams it down the ground. Mid-off is too wide and that's four all day!

full_text: Fred Klaassen to Rizwan, 2 runs, fuller on the pads, a bit of shape back in. Glanced to deep backward square leg and Rizwan is away with a couple of runs!
